commonIssues:
Telangana requires an Integrated Caste Certificate combining Caste, Nativity, and Date of Birth into one document, making the process more complex than other states.

How to fix:

  • Apply for the Integrated Certificate through TS MeeSeva Portal.
  • Provide SSC marks memo, Aadhaar card, and ration card as base documents.
  • Submit father's/grandfather's caste certificate as paternal proof.
  • Visit the nearest MeeSeva centre for assisted filing.

Server errors, document upload failures, and payment gateway issues on the MeeSeva portal.

How to fix:

  • Use Google Chrome (latest version) and clear cache before accessing.
  • Upload documents in JPEG/PDF under 200KB.
  • If payment failed but amount was deducted, raise a payment dispute on the portal.
  • Visit the nearest MeeSeva centre for in-person filing.

Applications pending at the Mandal Revenue Officer (MRO) level for extended periods.

How to fix:

  • Track status on MeeSeva Portal using your application number.
  • File a grievance at TS Grievance Portal if delayed beyond 15 days.
  • Escalate to the District Collector office or attend Praja Vani (public hearing).
  • Invoke the TS Right to Service Act for time-bound delivery.

Applications are sent to the Caste Verification Committee which conducts extensive field inquiries causing months of delay.

How to fix:

  • Keep all original documents ready for the committee visit.
  • Provide school records, temple records, and community elder testimonies proactively.
  • If committee hasn't visited within 30 days, follow up at the MRO office.
  • File a grievance at TS Grievance Portal.

Confusion regarding BC-A, BC-B, BC-C, BC-D, and BC-E sub-classifications unique to Telangana.

How to fix:

  • Check the official TS BC list from the TS BC Welfare Department.
  • Verify your specific sub-category (A/B/C/D/E) before applying.
  • Consult the MRO office for clarification on your category.
  • If wrong category was selected, apply for correction at the MRO office.

Errors in the issued certificate — misspelled names, wrong caste name, or incorrect mandal.

How to fix:

  • Apply for certificate correction at the MRO office with supporting documents.
  • Submit SSC memo, Aadhaar, and family certificates as evidence.
  • For online certificates, request re-issuance via MeeSeva Portal.
  • If refused, escalate to the District Collector.

Applications rejected without adequate reasons and re-application process being cumbersome.

How to fix:

  • Request a written reason for rejection from the MRO.
  • File an appeal with the Revenue Divisional Officer (RDO) within 30 days.
  • Lodge a grievance at TS Grievance Portal.
  • Seek free legal aid from DLSA — NALSA Portal.

MeeSeva centre operators sometimes charge more than the prescribed fee (₹35-₹50).

How to fix:

  • Check the official fee on MeeSeva Portal before visiting a centre.
  • Apply directly online to avoid centre charges.
  • Report overcharging to the District Collector or TS Grievance Portal.
  • Always collect a printed receipt showing the exact fee charged.
